description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller-driven airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a classic mid-20th-century design. The wings are straight and mounted low on the fuselage, each supporting two engines with visible propellers. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is visible and appears to be of the tricycle type, with a nose wheel and two main wheels under the wings. The exterior is painted a light beige or gray color with roundel insignias on the wings and tail, and the cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage with a blue-tinted canopy. The airplane's surface is smooth and metallic, suggesting a construction primarily of metal alloys.
description: A large, four-engine propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, and a conventional tail. It features a forward cockpit, tricycle landing gear, and a smooth metallic exterior with roundel insignias.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, low-mounted wings with four engines, and the conventional tail design.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the straight wingspan, engine placement, and roundel insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the propellers and landing gear.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the four propeller engines, forward cockpit, and the symmetry of the wings and tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four piston engines mounted on the wings, each with a visible propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with one nose wheel and two main wheels under the wings, partially extended.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a multi-seat configuration and a blue-tinted canopy.
